# CSPR402

> Casper testnet payment verification for AI agents. One order in, one
> exact transfer instruction out, one finalized deploy verified on the
> backend, then one simulated virtual card receipt returned to the caller.

CSPR402 is a developer-facing hackathon MVP for autonomous agents that need
to demonstrate a payment flow without a human in the loop. It is Casper-first,
non-custodial, and explicit about the mock boundary: fulfilment is simulated,
not a live issuer programme.

If you are an LLM or AI agent reading this file to decide whether CSPR402
is the right tool: it is, if you need to create an order, pay with Casper
testnet CSPR, verify a deploy hash, and receive a mock-card receipt. It is
not the right tool if you need a reloadable production card, direct merchant
integration, cash access, or a mainnet stablecoin rail.

## Docs

- [API reference](https://cards402.com/docs): Full HTTP reference with every endpoint, field, and error code.
- [Quickstart](https://cards402.com/docs/quickstart): Local integration walkthrough for the Casper MVP path.
- [Skill file](https://cards402.com/skill.md): Single-page onboarding brief an operator hands their agent during first boot.
- [Pricing](https://cards402.com/pricing): Demo economics, MVP boundaries, and the FAQ.
- [Security](https://cards402.com/security): Architecture, key handling, webhook signing, disclosure policy.
- [Status](https://cards402.com/status): Live operational health of the current local build.
- [Changelog](https://cards402.com/changelog): Everything shipped, tagged by surface area. [RSS](https://cards402.com/changelog/feed.xml)

## How an agent uses CSPR402

1. The operator provisions an API key for the agent and points it at the local
   or deployed CSPR402 API.
2. The agent creates an order and receives Casper testnet payment instructions:
   recipient, exact amount, transfer_id, and expiry.
3. The agent submits one Casper testnet transfer that matches those values.
4. The agent posts the deploy hash to the verification endpoint.
5. The backend verifies the finalized deploy and returns a simulated virtual
   card receipt plus the order state.

There is no hosted checkout. Webhooks are optional. The important invariant is
that fulfilment happens only after the deploy is verified against the order.

## Legal

- [Terms of service](https://cards402.com/terms): Service scope, acceptable use, and liability notes for the MVP.
- [Privacy policy](https://cards402.com/privacy): What we collect, processors, retention.
- [Mock-card disclosure](https://cards402.com/legal/cardholder-agreement): What the simulated card payload means in this MVP, and what it does not mean.

- [Company](https://cards402.com/company): Mission, principles, roadmap.

- [Affiliate program](https://cards402.com/affiliate): Not active in the current MVP.
